blindspots: **CMS Prior Auth revenue quantification** — Federal mandate is live but no one (including WAY) has quantified the revenue impact. First data expected Q1-Q2 2026 earnings. This is the biggest unknown and the key catalyst.

**Iodine clinical AI attach rate** — 35% client overlap creates cross-sell opportunity, but actual attachment rate to existing payment clients is unknown. Monitor via: quarterly commentary on Iodine pipeline.

**Bullet maturity refinancing risk** — $1,438.7M due Oct 2029. Market conditions 2-3 years out will determine refi cost. If rates stay elevated, significant interest expense increase possible. Monitor via: credit markets, WAY's credit rating trajectory.

**Payer automation readiness** — CMS rule requires payer compliance, but actual payer technology readiness varies. Slow payer adoption delays WAY's revenue uplift. No public tracking of payer compliance rates.

cyclical.context: Healthcare IT capex generally resilient but not immune to macro. Bifurcation — patient-payment softer, core provider-solutions intact. Monitor Q2 (guide softened to flat-to-+1% sequential vs prior 1-3%)
cyclical.current: **Q1 2026: Patient-payment segment (~25% of total rev) faced "hospital financing difficulties" + "print-to-digital shift" + "payer pushback" (mgmt: "not competitive or product-driven"). Provider solutions (75% of rev) growing 13-14% organic — resilient.**
cyclical.name: Hospital capex environment

cyclical.context: Expanding with scale; Q1 above FY25, at FY26 guide. Iodine integration noise contained. Judge on trailing 4Q basis
cyclical.current: **Q1 2026: 43% (vs 42% FY2025; in-line with 43% FY2026 guide).** UFCF $90M (67% EBITDA conversion).
cyclical.name: Adj EBITDA margin

cyclical.context: Technical, not fundamental. Each secondary reduces overhang for future
cyclical.current: Overhang from PE sponsor selling down. Creates near-term price pressure. (No new Q1 data — keep unchanged.)
cyclical.name: PE sponsor secondary offerings

noiseFilter: **PE sponsor secondary offerings** — Each secondary reduces overhang. Price pressure is mechanical, not fundamental. Don't confuse selling by financial sponsors with thesis deterioration.
**Goodwill impairment speculation** — 91.7% of assets is goodwill. Only matters if growth materially slows. At 38% subscription growth, impairment is not imminent.
**Single-quarter EBITDA margin dips** — Iodine integration will create near-term noise in margins. Track underlying margin ex-integration costs.

**Epic competitive announcements** — Epic is large but WAY's value prop is cross-EHR (500+ integrations). Single-EHR solutions don't compete with cross-platform workflow. Track actual EHR switching, not announcements.

structural.current: Live Jan 1, 2026; WAY is incumbent processor for ~60% of US patients. **Q1 2026 call: management did NOT quantify CMS-specific revenue impact or payer compliance %. Blindspot persists.** Closest proxy: "approximately 40% of new bookings" AI-driven (#3) — broader than CMS but directionally consistent.
structural.name: CMS Prior Authorization Rule adoption
structural.threshold: Rule diluted/delayed = bear case; accelerated adoption = bull case. **First read attempted Q1 2026; pushed to Q2 2026 (~Aug) — quantification gap is itself a soft signal: incumbent processor isn't yet seeing material rule-driven uplift.**

structural.current: **Q1 2026: Subscription rev $172M (+38% YoY headline, +14% organic).** Total Q1 rev $314M (+22% YoY, 11% organic). FY26 guide reaffirmed $1.274-1.294B (16-18%); Q2 sequential softer (flat to +1% vs prior guide 1-3%) due to patient-payment pressure.
structural.direction: ↑ Positive (Q1 confirmed +38% headline / +14% organic; above >35% accel threshold on headline, in-range on organic)
structural.name: Subscription revenue growth (YoY)
structural.threshold: <25% = deceleration concern; <15% = structural slowdown; >35% = AI/CMS acceleration

structural.current: **Q1 2026: "roughly 40% of new bookings" driven by AI-powered capabilities (up from 30-35% FY2025).** "Nearly 40% of revenue is generated by AI-embedded workflows" (CEO Hawkins). 150+ production AI models (AltitudeAI).
structural.direction: ↑ **Approaching upper threshold — AT 40% boundary** (not yet >40%; one more quarterly tick crosses it)
structural.name: AI bookings as % of new bookings
structural.threshold: <20% = AI not differentiating; >40% = AI becoming core value prop

structural.current: **Q1 2026: Net leverage 2.7x (improved from 3.0x at end-2025; 0.3-turn improvement in a single quarter).** $1,438.7M bullet maturity due Oct 2029 unchanged. UFCF $90M Q1 (67% EBITDA conversion).
structural.direction: ↓ Improving **faster than 1-turn/yr trajectory** — 0.2x from <2.5x deleverage-on-track threshold
structural.name: Net debt / adj EBITDA leverage
structural.threshold: >3.5x = leverage concern; <2.5x = deleverage thesis on track; approaching maturity without refinancing = risk

structural.current: **Q1 2026: Integration "running ahead of plan" (CEO). Iodine clinical data covers ~1/3 of US hospital discharges annually — provides AI training corpus addressing "convergence of financial and clinical data" (CEO-described "1 of the largest unmet needs in the revenue cycle").** Cross-sell pipeline qualifying — WAY started Q2 with "largest qualified pipeline of new and cross-sell upsell opportunities in our history."
structural.name: Iodine integration / cross-sell
structural.threshold: Failed integration = impairment; cross-sell attach >20% = revenue acceleration

structural.current: **Q1 2026: 111%** (down 1pp from FY2025 112% but "slightly above historical 108%-110% range" per CFO).
structural.name: Net revenue retention (NRR)

trendLog.action: No action — Q1 2026 earnings first read on CMS impact
trendLog.assessment: All indicators on track. CMS rule live but no revenue data yet. Subscription +38%, AI bookings 30-35%, leverage improving. Iodine in early integration.

trendLog.action: No structural breach. Iodine ahead of plan is material positive for #5. CMS revenue quantification (#1) still pending — Q1 2026 earnings (~May) is first read.
trendLog.assessment: All indicators confirmed positive. Subscription growth strong. Iodine exceeded expectations. NRR 112% / GRR 97% = best-in-class. CMS Prior Auth: adoption tracking underway, no payer compliance % data yet. Record bookings suggest Q1 2026 will be strong.
trendLog.new: Q4: Rev $303.5M (+24%), organic 12%, Iodine $31M, EBITDA 43%. FY2025: Rev $1,099M (+17%), organic 13%, EBITDA $462.1M (42%), NRR 112%, GRR 97%. Record Q4 bookings: 15-20 >$1M (2x historic avg). **Iodine integration ahead of plan** — accelerated roadmap by ~2 years, 1,000+ hospitals added, >90% of $14M synergies in 2026. Auth Accelerate: 70% time reduction, >$15B denials prevented. 2026 guide: $1.274-1.294B (16-18%), organic ~10%, EBITDA ≥40%.

trendLog.action: No CHANGELOG mandatory trigger fires (no V-dim shift, no allocation/band change, no scenario probability re-weighting). **Q2 sequential softness + #1 quantification gap raise discipline question — flag for Q2 review.** Live $19.72 (Source: prices.py 2026-05-13) — **below bear-case floor $20-22** since at least late-April; monitor whether price discount reflects #1 gap or broader sector de-rating. Set #1 next-read expectation = Q2 2026 earnings (~Aug 4-7) + Analyst Day Aug 25 (CMS-monetization disclosure highest-impact open question).
trendLog.assessment: **No structural threshold breach.** #2 subscription headline above >35% accel threshold; organic 14% in-range. #3 AI bookings AT 40% boundary (not yet >40% breach — one more quarterly tick crosses). #4 leverage faster-than-trajectory (0.2x from <2.5x deleverage-on-track threshold). #5 Iodine sustained ahead-of-plan (4th consecutive quarter). #6 NRR -1pp but in-range. #8 margin at FY26 guide. **Soft signal: #1 quantification gap is itself meaningful — if incumbent ~60% processor isn't seeing material CMS-driven uplift by Q1 2026, either the rule is operating at lower revenue intensity than thesis assumed OR uplift is back-end-loaded to H2 2026.**
trendLog.new: Q1 rev $314M (+22% YoY, **organic 11%**); Subscription $172M (**+38% headline, +14% organic**); Volume-based $139M (+7%); Adj EBITDA $135M (+26%, **43% margin**); UFCF $90M (67% conversion); **Net leverage 2.7x (down from 3.0x — single-quarter 0.3-turn improvement)**; **NRR 111%** (-1pp vs FY25 but within 108-110% historical band per CFO); **AI bookings ~40% of new** (up from 30-35%); "nearly 40% of revenue is AI-embedded workflows." Iodine "running ahead of plan" — clinical data on ~1/3 of US hospital discharges. **42 new $100k+ clients (total 1,433, +15% YoY).** FY26 guide reaffirmed $1.274-1.294B / Adj EBITDA $530-540M. **Q2 sequential guide softened: flat-to-+1% (vs prior 1-3%)** due to patient-payment-segment pressure (hospital financing difficulties, print-to-digital, payer pushback — "not competitive or product-driven" per mgmt). Started Q2 with **"largest qualified pipeline in our history."** **CMS Prior Auth #1: management did NOT quantify CMS-specific revenue impact or payer compliance %** — Q1 was expected first read; quantification pushed to Q2 (~Aug) or later. **Analyst Day announced Tuesday Aug 25 2026.**
